_schemaNote: No formal JSON Schema for this file (informal shape — see method below); deliberately not using the "$schema" key so editors don't try to fetch it as a real schema URL. The flat top-level `correctness`/`speed`/`performance`/`regressionTolerancePercent` keys are the machine-read contract that the CI guards in .github/workflows/ci.yml and agent-image.yml consume — keep them stable. The `gateShape`/`gateCorrectness`/`gateSpeed`/`gatePerformance`/`reproducibilityNotes`/`environment` sections are the richer human-readable capture (relay#74) layered on top; they add detail and do not change the machine contract.

capturedFrom.note: Working tree for this capture is HEAD of main at capture time (verified via `git merge-base` before measuring).

method: correctness: `npx eslint . -f json` (sum of errorCount/warningCount across all files)
and `pnpm run typecheck` (count of `error TS` lines) run on packages/*/src against
commit 2c9b3fa4 — the same commit this file was added on top of, i.e. the gate SHAPE
this baseline describes already includes the fixes from relay#75 (typecheck wired into
CI, eslint --max-warnings enforced, continue-on-error removed). Correctness counts were
0 eslint errors / 57 eslint warnings (pre-existing `any` usage in test files) / 0
typecheck errors — no debt was frozen beyond those pre-existing warnings.
speed: GitHub Actions run 29951182082 (ci.yml, job "build", push of PR #73 to main,
2026-07-22T19:29:36Z-2026-07-22T19:30:08Z = 32s) is the last representative main-branch
gate run BEFORE this change. That run's gate shape (lint with continue-on-error, no
typecheck step) is cheaper than the shape landing in this commit, so the 32s figure is
a conservative floor, not a like-for-like number — the 30% tolerance absorbs the added
typecheck step (~2-3s locally) plus normal CI variance. Re-baseline once a representative
run of the NEW gate shape is available (see note below).
performance.runnerMinutes: derived from the same run (single ubuntu-latest runner job,
so runner-minutes ~= wall-clock minutes for this job): 32s / 60 ~= 0.6min (rounded up).
performance.dockerImageSizeBytes: not captured in this pass — no docker CLI available in
the sandbox this baseline was authored in, and .github/workflows/agent-image.yml did not
previously report image size. This change adds a size-report + guard step to that
workflow; its first run will log the measured size for a human to fold into a follow-up
baseline-update commit. Until then the guard treats a null baseline as 'record only'.
performance.cacheHitRate: single-sample observation from the same run (the pnpm
actions/setup-node cache hit its primary key). Not a rolling rate — needs several
historical runs to become meaningful; tracked as a follow-up.
methodNarrative: Two independent measurements against the same commit (2c9b3fa, HEAD of main at capture time, matching this branch's merge-base with origin/main). (1) CI: read actual GitHub Actions job/step timestamps for the 5 most recent push-to-main runs of ci.yml via `gh api repos/toon-protocol/relay/actions/runs/<id>/jobs` and `.../timing`, and grepped each run's `build`/`Devbox Environment Validation` job logs for 'Cache hit for:' vs 'Cache not found'. (2) Local: ran the 4 gate commands against a fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, timing each with `time`. Reproduce locally with: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm -r run build && eslint . ; pnpm run typecheck ; vitest run` (build must run before typecheck — see reproducibilityNotes below).

ciWiring.lint: as of relay#75 (already on main when this detail was folded in), `pnpm lint` runs as a hard gate with `--max-warnings` sourced from correctness.eslintWarnings (no longer `continue-on-error: true`). Recorded here because the original relay#74 capture predates that fix.
ciWiring.typecheck: as of relay#75, `pnpm run typecheck` is a hard CI gate step; before #75 it existed as a script (added in #66) but no CI step invoked it.
ciWiring.test: run as `pnpm -r test --if-present` in the `build` job (blocking).
ciWiring.build: run as `pnpm -r build` in the `build` job (blocking), and again standalone in the `devbox-validate` job's smoke build.

reproducibilityNotes: `pnpm run typecheck` depends on `packages/relay` being built first: `packages/bls` imports the workspace package `@toon-protocol/relay` by its published entry point, which only exists once `packages/relay/dist/` has been generated by `pnpm -r run build`. Running typecheck against a clean checkout before building fails with `TS2307: Cannot find module '@toon-protocol/relay'` at packages/bls/src/entrypoint.ts:44 — a false FAIL from command-ordering, not a real type error. Verified by reproducing it here (rm -rf packages/{relay,bls}/dist; pnpm run typecheck fails; pnpm -r run build; pnpm run typecheck then passes clean).
CI's `build` job runs lint -> build -> test in that order; the typecheck number is sourced from a local run (see gateShape.ciWiring.typecheck for the CI wiring landed by relay#75).

environment.ciRunner: ubuntu-24.04 (GitHub-hosted, public repo — Actions minutes unmetered)

gateSpeed.description: CI wall-clock time for ci.yml. Both jobs (`build`, `Devbox Environment Validation`) run in parallel; wall-clock as experienced by a PR author is the whole-workflow duration, not either job alone. NOTE: the machine-read `speed.gateWallClockSeconds` (32s) is the conservative floor the CI guard enforces against; the numbers below are the fuller multi-sample capture.

local.description: Sequential run of the 4 gate commands against a fresh install, on the capture sandbox (4 vCPU / 15GB — not representative of CI's per-job isolation, provided for local-reproduction reference only).

gatePerformance.description: Runner cost & cache efficiency. relay is a PUBLIC repo, so GitHub bills $0 regardless of runner-minutes used (the Actions API always reports billable.UBUNTU.total_ms=0) — raw job-second totals are recorded as a cost proxy, not a bill. The machine-read `performance` block above is the guard's contract; this section is the fuller capture.

runnerMinutes.unit: raw job-seconds summed across both ci.yml jobs (build + Devbox Environment Validation), NOT GitHub-billed minutes
runnerMinutes.billedMinutesNote: Public repo — GitHub-hosted Actions minutes are free; `gh api .../actions/runs/<id>/timing` reports billable.UBUNTU.total_ms=0 for every sampled run.

cacheHitRate.pnpmDependencyCache: 5/5 (100%) — action: actions/setup-node@v4 with cache: pnpm, keyed on pnpm-lock.yaml, in the `build` job.

dockerImageSize.note: The .sandcastle/Dockerfile agent image (sandcastle:relay) had no prior run that recorded its size. The 'Gate performance no-regression guard (image size)' step in .github/workflows/agent-image.yml (landed by relay#75) reports and records the size on its next run; the machine-read `performance.dockerImageSizeBytes` stays null until a human folds that number into a follow-up baseline-update commit (the guard treats null as record-only).

gateCorrectness.description: Current violation counts against a clean checkout of capturedFromCommit. These are the frozen baseline the epic (toon-protocol/toon-meta#210) ratchets toward zero in follow-up slices — this issue only captures the number. The machine-read `correctness` block above mirrors these counts. relay has no existing correctness debt to freeze.

lint.note: All 57 warnings are in test files (non-null assertions and `any` casts in test fixtures/mocks).

typecheck.note: 0 errors when run after `pnpm -r run build` (see reproducibilityNotes).
